heading: Checkout Service 500 Errors due to Redis Connection Pool Exhaustion
summary: A spike in 500 errors on the checkout-service was observed, correlating with Redis connection pool exhaustion. Investigation revealed that a recent PR (#402) merged on 2026-06-07T08:20:00Z, which aimed to optimize Redis pool timeouts and idle slots, inadvertently led to the pool becoming exhausted. This resulted in `JedisConnectionException` errors and a cascade of 500 internal server errors for the checkout service.

sources.name: PROMETHEUS
sources.description: Prometheus metrics showing a sharp increase in HTTP 500 errors for the checkout-service, starting around 1780811594.

sources.name: KIBANA
sources.description: Kibana logs indicating `JedisConnectionException` errors related to Redis connection pool exhaustion, occurring around the time of the Prometheus spike.

hits.stack_trace:
redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisConnectionException: Could not get a resource from the pool
 at redis.clients.jedis.util.Pool.getResource

hits.message: Pool exhaustion detected. Active connections: 50, Idle connections: 0
hits.stack_trace: redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisConnectionException: Timeout waiting for idle object in pool

sources.name: GITHUB
sources.description: Details of the GitHub Pull Request (#402) that modified Redis connection pool configurations, merged shortly before the incident.

pull_requests.title: perf(redis): Optimize session validation lookup by adjusting pool timeouts
pull_requests.diff_summary: Modified application.properties. Decreased spring.redis.jedis.pool.max-wait down to 50ms and lowered max-idle slots.
